thought_beat_text: The fingerprint in the clay is his now—he can't stop tracing it with his eyes, the way the ridge loops wrong, too deliberate, too placed—and the worst part is that the mission still needs to be real, needs to hold weight, but he can feel the foam-core give beneath the surface every time he presses. He keeps rehearsing the next step as if momentum will seal the crack, but the crack knows what momentum is.
residue_summary: He can see the seam clearly now and has chosen not to say it aloud, which means the silence is load-bearing and he knows it.
image_hint: close-up of a thumb pressed against painted cardboard, the surface dimpling inward under stage light
audio_hint: low drone with a single dry creak, unresolved
